crack_321.note: 3-2-1 crack $54.7/bbl: Wide — product demand outpacing crude. Refiners run harder → crude demand grows.

brent_wti.note: Brent-WTI $3.42/bbl: Normal range — US exports flowing, North Sea intact

nci_crack.label: CRACKS_VERY_WIDE
nci_crack.interpretation: NCI Crack +8.8 (CRACKS_VERY_WIDE): Exceptional refinery margins. Strong crude demand pull.

signals: High refinery util (>92%) → physical demand strong → backwardation pressure
Cushing only 26% full → tight prompt crude → backwardation
Normal Brent-WTI spread → US exports flowing → balanced
forward_curve.note: Physical market signals point to prompt tightness. M1-M2 likely in backwardation.
forward_curve.context: Full curve shape requires M1-M12 strip data (Day 9 FastAPI will add this). Current estimate based on Cushing util, refinery runs, and Brent-WTI spread.

seasonal.note: Peak US driving + EM A/C power demand. Highest crack spread month historically.
